On March 8, the U.S. Senate passed the Lend-
Lease Act. The support for the U.S. has
increased in England and Holland.

The United States will not fight in this war,
but we must serve as an armory for the nations
of democracy.

The German battleship, Bismarck, was sunk in
the Atlantic on May 27.

Superb! Our Royal Navy sunk Hitler's proud
Bismarck! The biggest threat in the Atlantic
has been destroyed.

Although we lost the Bismarck, we still have
our sea wolves - the U-boats. Paralyze the
British sea transport and starve them.

On June 22, the Third Reich breached the non-
aggression pact with Moscow and launched the
Barbarossa Operation to embark on the invasion
of the USSR.

I declare the launch of the Barbarossa
Operation! With blitzkrieg by our Panzer,
Moscow will surrender before winter.

My fellow citizens! We must stand up and
fight for our mother country! We must not let
invaders take a single grain of wheat or a drop
of gasoline!

On July 28, the Japanese army began occupying
Southern Indochina.

The German-controlled French government has
accepted our demand to occupy Indochina. This
will secure us a foothold to the south.

In response on the Japanese aggression in
French Indochina, the U.S. government froze all
Japanese assets in the U.S. The Japanese product-
ivity has dropped by 10%.

The Japanese are disturbing peace in Asia by
imposing a selfish rationale. To stop their
aggression, we will have to take sanctions
against them.

The U.S. imposed an oil embargo against Japan.
The Japanese productivity has dropped by 10%.

As long as Japan maintains its armed diplo-
macy, we won't be able to continue the peace
conferences.

Our fuel supply will last for only one more
year. We must quickly decide whether we'll fall
with or without fighting.

The Japanese government has concluded that war
is inevitable unless there is a breakthrough in
the negotiations with the U.S. by early October.

Mainland China is the lifeline of our army.
It's absolutely impossible to accept America's
demand for our complete withdrawal. I'm afraid
war is inevitable.

The negotiation with Japan is coming to a
close. With our economic blockade pressing them
hard, it's a matter of time that they'll give in
to us.

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a
report on the statements from foreign leaders.

We welcome Japan's diplomatic efforts. Depend-
ing on the circumstances, we may approve Japan's
interest in the Pacific.

Our country has no intention to intervene
in the political and military affairs in the
Pacific. We will maintain neutrality.

The United States is our comrade and the only
major power capable of overturning Fascism.

I welcome Japan's diplomatic efforts. If we
can regain our land from the Germans, we will
not hesitate to support Japan.

We will stand neutral regarding the conflicts
in the Pacific. We have no intention to make
political and military interventions.

The United States is a symbol of freedom. Only
the United States will be able to restore the
Dutch land now occupied by the Germans.

It's time to end the conflict with the
Japanese. If they support our rights and
interests, why not work with them for the
development of Asia!

We condemn the aggression of Imperial Japan.
If the United States are to stop them, we will
give our support.

Amid the fight against the Germans, we do not
wish to provoke the Japanese and their army in
Manchuria. We value friendly relations with
Japan.

Amid the fight against the Germans, we can't
afford to spread our forces to the Far East. We
will stand neutral for the time being.

We are struggling against the Germans. We ask
the U.S. to open the second front and press
Germany.

Japan is an important friend as well as a
former ally in the Tripartite Pact. I urge Japan
to declare war against the British and the
Russians as soon as possible.

The goal of Germany is the unification of
Europe. War in the Pacific is not our concern.

It is not in our best interest to face the
U.S. military power in Europe. Depending on the
circumstances, we may choose to ally with the
U.S.

The State Department issued a report on the
statements from foreign leaders.

Estimation of the Budget Expenditures 

       New Warship   :  1000/ship           Base Upkeep :   500/base
       Warship Repair:   250/ship           New Harbor  :  3000/each
       New Aircraft  :    10/plane          New Airfield:  2000/each
       Division      :    10/unit           Technology  :   500/topic
       Foreign Expen.:  1000/nation         Government  :  minimum 5000

Estimation of the Material Expenditures

       Fleet Usage   :  3000/fleet          A.F. Usage  :   250/base
       New Warship   :  2500/ship           Base Upkeep :   500/base
       Warship Repair:   250/ship           New Harbor  :  5000/each
       New Aircraft  :    20/plane          New Airfield:  3000/each
       Division      :   150/unit           National    :  minimum 10000

Drafting Forces

 Navy  : Assign up to 20 reserve units to the Marines.

 Army  : Assign up to 30 units to the army reserves.

 GNP   : You may draft 1 unit for every 1 point of GNP when your
         industry is 50 or higher.

         You may draft 1 unit for every 2 points of GNP when your
         industry is 49 or lower.

         The maximum number of people you may draft is equal to your
         GNP minus 20.

 Europe: The number of units sent to fight in the European front.

Technology

 Spy Tech.  : The technology of information gathering. An increase in spy
              technology improves the capacity of electronic weapons and
              the success rate of searching for enemies.

 Naval Tech.: The technology of developing advanced warships. An increase in
              naval technology shortens the time to repair or build ships.

 Aeronautics: The technology of developing advanced aircraft. It affects the
              rate of determining successful hits in an air battle.

 Weaponry   : The technology of building new weapons. It affects the rate of
              determining successful hits in battles.

 Industry   : The technology of producing industrial outputs. It affects the
              costs to build and repair weapons.

Base Transports: Ships used to transport materials from
                  the bases to the mainland on the first
                  day of each month. Up to 200 units of
                  materials may be loaded per transport.

Base Materials: Each base needs materials for fleet and
                 land force supplies, warship repairs,
                 and base air force mobility points.

We promise you a sweeping
victory, Commander!1. Occupy Hong Kong, Manila, Singapore, Batavia, and Balikpapan by 2/15/42.2. Destroy the British Royal Navy by 2/15/42.3. Establish supply routes from Japan to Batavia by 2/15/42.1. Sink 10 or more aircraft carriers or battleships by 2/15/42.2. Sink 30 or more warships by 2/15/42.3. Hold Singapore and occupy Saigon, Hanoi, and Bangkok by 2/15/42.1. Occupy Midway and Attu Island by June 18, 1942.2. Sink 3 or more aircraft carriers by June 18, 1942.3. Sink an aircraft carrier and occupy Midway by June 18, 1942.1. Sink 4 or more aircraft carriers, or 6 or more battleships by 6/18/1942.2. Sink 20 or more warships by 6/18/1942.3. Hold the Midway Base and occupy Wake by 6/18/1942.1. Occupy at least two bases in Guadalcanal, E. Santo, or Fiji by 9/28/42.2. Occupy Port Moresby by 9/28/42.3. Hold the Truk Base and sink 30 or more warships by 9/28/42.1. Sink 8 or more aircraft carriers or battleships by 9/28/42.2. Hold the P. Moresby Base and sink 30 or more enemy warships by 9/28/42.3. Occupy at least two bases in Truk, Rabaul, or Wewak by 9/28/42.1. Sink 10 or more aircraft carriers or battleships by 6/9/44.2. Hold the Saipan and Guam bases, and sink 30 or more warships by 6/9/44.3. Hold Saipan and occupy the Marshall and Gilbert Bases by 6/9/44.1. Occupy three bases in Saipan, Guam, Palau, or Biak by July 9, 1944.2. Sink 3 or more aircraft carriers and occupy Saipan by July 9, 1944.3. Sink 10 or more carriers or battleships by July 9, 1944.1. Sink 10 or more aircraft carriers or battleships by Nov. 23, 1944.2. Hold the Manila Base and sink 30 or more warships by Nov. 23, 1944.3. Occupy Saipan and Guam by Nov. 23, 1944.1. Occupy Manila, Davao, and Palau by 11/23/44.2. Sink 10 or more carriers or battleships by 11/23/44.3. Sink 20 or more warships including 1 dreadnought battleship by 11/23/44.1. Sink 8 or more aircraft carriers, or 10 or more battleships by 4/11/45.2. Sink 30 or more warships by 4/11/45.3. Establish supply routes between Japan and Truk by 4/11/45.1. Occupy Iwo Jima and Naha by 4/11/1945.2. Sink the dreadnought battleship, Yamato, and occupy Naha by 4/11/1945.3. Annihilate the Japanese fleet by 4/11/1945.
